behaviors.description: SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to correct sum of range
behaviors.justification: Pure computational logic: formula evaluator reads cell values from a range and applies an aggregation function. No external dependencies, OS behavior, or cross-module coordination. A mock of cell lookup would not hide failures because the test operates on real cell objects in memory.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test creates cells A1:A10 with known numeric values, sets a cell to =SUM(A1:A10), and verifies the result equals the expected sum. Tests both the range parsing logic and the SUM function implementation.

behaviors.description: Cascaded formula references (B1=A1*2, C1=B1+1) produce correct result
behaviors.justification: Tests formula evaluator's reference resolution and cascading evaluation in pure in-memory computation. The evaluator must resolve B1's formula before using its result in C1. While multiple cells are involved, no external infrastructure or real module separation is required—it's testing the core logic of the evaluator.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test sets A1=5, B1=A1*2, C1=B1+1, evaluates C1, and verifies result is 11. Confirms the evaluator correctly resolves intermediate references before dependent evaluation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1→B1, B1→A1) detected and returns error
behaviors.justification: Tests the circular dependency detection algorithm in isolation. This is purely graph-traversal logic with no external dependencies. The algorithm must complete without infinite loops and correctly identify cycles.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test sets A1=B1 and B1=A1, then attempts to evaluate either cell and verifies a circular reference error is returned immediately without looping or crashing.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error
behaviors.justification: Pure function logic operating on in-memory lookup table data. No external dependencies, OS behavior, or cross-module coordination. Tests the VLOOKUP function's search and error-handling logic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test creates a lookup table, calls VLOOKUP with an existing key and verifies correct row value returned; calls with non-existent key and verifies error is returned. Tests both success and failure paths.

behaviors.description: IF with nested conditions (IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, ...), ...)) returns correct value
behaviors.justification: Tests IF function's condition evaluation and branch selection logic. Pure deterministic computation with no external dependencies. Condition evaluation order and nesting are handled by the evaluator's core logic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test sets A1 to different numeric values (5, 15, 25) and verifies nested IF correctly returns 'low', 'medium', or 'high' based on branching logic. Tests nested condition evaluation and result selection.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator, skips empty cells (no blanks inserted)
behaviors.justification: Tests CONCATENATE function logic for string joining and special-case handling of empty cells. Pure string manipulation with no external dependencies or cross-module coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test creates cells with mixed content (text, numbers, empty), calls CONCATENATE with separator, and verifies output contains correct concatenation without spurious blank strings or extra separators.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (Sheet2!A1) resolves correctly when referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: Requires coordinating multiple sheet objects within workbook state and testing sheet lookup + reference resolution together. Real failures would occur if: sheets aren't properly isolated/accessible, sheet names parse incorrectly, or workbook doesn't maintain sheet references. Mocking the sheet lookup would hide these integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test creates a workbook with multiple actual sheets, references a cell from Sheet2 in a Sheet1 formula (e.g., =Sheet2!A1+1), verifies correct value is resolved. Also tests that sheets remain isolated and correct sheet is returned on lookup.

behaviors.description: When a cell's value changes, dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order
behaviors.justification: Tests change propagation engine and dependency graph ordering—requires coordinating multiple cells, their dependencies, and recalculation sequence. Mocking the recalculation logic would hide failures in the topological sort algorithm or change propagation mechanism. Real failures occur when dependents are recalculated out of order or some recalcs are skipped.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test creates a dependency chain (A1 → B1, B1 → C1, C1 → D1), changes A1 to a new value, and verifies all downstream formulas recalculate in correct topological order. Monitors recalculation sequence to ensure no cell is evaluated before its dependencies are resolved.

behaviors.description: Undo restores formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: Requires coordinating undo state management across multiple dependent cells and verifying both formula text and computed values are restored. Mocking the undo mechanism would hide real failures in state restoration or incomplete dependent value caching. Must test the full undo-stack integration with actual cell state.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test creates a formula with dependent cells, edits the formula to a new expression, verifies dependent cells recalculate, then executes undo and verifies both the formula text and all dependent cell values are restored to their pre-edit state.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and load back: identical formulas, values, and floating-point precision
behaviors.justification: Requires actual file I/O to the custom binary format—real OS behavior that mocks cannot reveal. Real failures occur from: binary format bugs (endianness, encoding), precision loss during float serialization, file system errors (permissions, partial writes), or OS-specific file handle behavior. Integration testing with in-memory serialization would miss actual file I/O issues.
behaviors.test_description: System test creates a workbook with formulas including floating-point values, writes it to disk using the binary format, reads it back, and verifies: all formula text matches, all cell values match, and floating-point precision is preserved (no rounding errors from serialization/deserialization).
